Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.toml

Class TomlParser

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.Parser
org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser
org.apache.juneau.marshall.toml.TomlParser
All Implemented Interfaces:
RecordReadable
public class TomlParser extends ReaderParser implements RecordReadable
Parses TOML v1.0.0 text into POJOs.

Handles Content-Type: application/toml

Limitations compared to JSON

TOML is a configuration format with a more restricted type system than JSON. The following features supported by JsonParser are not available or limited in TOML:

  • Null values — TOML has no null type. Absent keys yield null for object properties. The parser treats the configured nullValue string (e.g. <NULL>) as Java null when it appears as a value.
  • Non-string map keys — TOML keys are always strings. Non-string key types (e.g. Map<Integer,String>) are converted via convertAttrToType. Null keys are serialized as the string null and converted back to Java null during parsing.
  • Polymorphic types — Parsing to interfaces or abstract classes requires a bean dictionary when no _type discriminator is present in the document.
  • Duration — TOML has no native duration type. Quoted ISO-8601 duration strings (e.g. "PT1H30M") are parsed to Duration via Iso8601Utils.
  • Year and YearMonth — Bare Year (4-digit integer) parses as Long, not Year. Use quoted strings (e.g. "2024") or yyyy-MM for YearMonth; swaps may be needed for full compatibility.
Example:

// Parse TOML into a JsonMap String toml = "name = \"myapp\"\nport = 8080"; JsonMap parsed = TomlParser.DEFAULT.parse(toml, JsonMap.class); // Parse into a bean MyConfig config = TomlParser.DEFAULT.parse(toml, MyConfig.class); // Parse into a parameterized type (e.g. Map with type args) Map<String, Object> map = TomlParser.DEFAULT.parse(toml, Map.class, String.class, Object.class); // Use the marshaller for convenience parsed = Toml.to(toml, JsonMap.class);

Example input (Map of name/age):

name = "Alice" age = 30

Complex (nested table + array):

name = "Alice" age = 30 tags = ["a", "b", "c"] [address] street = "123 Main St" city = "Boston" state = "MA"

See Also:
  • TOML v1.0.0 Specification

    • createSession

      public TomlParserSession.Builder createSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class ReaderParser
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • copy

      public TomlParser.Builder copy()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Creates a builder from this context object.

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      Returns:

    • parseRecords

      public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator that opens a RecordReader over the input using default session arguments (mirrors Parser.parse(Object, Class)).

      The real implementation lives on TomlParserSession.parseRecords(Object). Callers that need request-derived configuration (locale, timezone, schema, swaps) should call createSession() and invoke TomlParserSession.parseRecords(Object) on the built session instead.

      Specified by:
      parseRecords in interface RecordReadable
      Parameters:
      input - The input.
      Returns:
      A new RecordReader cursor.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.

    • isRecordStreaming

      public boolean isRecordStreaming()
      Description copied from interface: RecordReadable
      Streaming-quality signal queryable before opening a cursor.
      Specified by:
      isRecordStreaming in interface RecordReadable
      Returns:
      true if RecordReadable.parseRecords(Object) returns an O(1)-memory streaming cursor; false for buffered (whole-value) formats backed by RecordAdapter.
